text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER

SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
DATE OF REPORT (FINAL): [PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Gastroenterology / Hepatopancreaticobiliary Surgery
REFERRING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED]
CLINICAL HISTORY: 66-year-old female presenting with 10-week history of progressive painless jaundice, 14-pound weight loss, epigastric pain radiating to the back, and Courvoisier sign on examination. CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreatic protocol demonstrated a 3.8 cm hypoenhancing mass in the pancreatic head and uncinate process with upstream pancreatic ductal dilation (7 mm) and common bile duct dilation (16 mm), abutting the SMA over 180 degrees and involving the SMV with focal narrowing. Findings are consistent with borderline resectable pancreatic head mass. CA 19-9 1,204 U/mL. EUS-guided biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN ID: SP-[REDACTED]-A

TYPE OF SPECIMEN:
EUS-guided fine needle biopsy (FNB), pancreatic head mass; 22-gauge Franseen needle; 3 passes performed with on-site cytologic evaluation confirming adequacy.

PROCEDURE:
Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS)-guided fine needle biopsy of the pancreatic head mass using a linear-array echoendoscope via transgastric approach. Real-time EUS guidance confirmed needle placement within the hypoechoic mass in the head/uncinate process. Three passes were made with a 22-gauge Franseen-tip needle. Rapid on-site evaluation (ROSE) by cytopathology was performed after the first pass and confirmed the presence of adequate cellularity with atypical glandular cells. Material was submitted as formalin-fixed cell block and smears for histologic and cytologic evaluation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION:

Received in formalin, labeled with patient name and "EUS FNB pancreatic head," are three core tissue fragments. The largest core measures 0.9 cm in length x 0.1 cm in diameter; additional cores measure 0.6 cm and 0.4 cm in greatest dimension respectively. The tissue is pale tan-white and firm with a slightly gritty texture. All material is submitted entirely in one cassette for histologic processing.

Also received separately, labeled "FNB cell block, pancreatic head," is a cell block pellet processed as a formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded cell block. Representative sections are submitted.

Total tissue submitted: Cassette A1 (all cores, entirely submitted). Cell block: Cassette A2.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Histologic sections of the core needle biopsy demonstrate fibrous and desmoplastic stromal tissue infiltrated by atypical epithelial elements. The neoplastic cells are arranged in irregularly shaped glandular structures, cribriform nests, and occasional single infiltrative cells dispersed within a densely fibrotic, hypocellular desmoplastic stroma. Individual tumor cells exhibit enlarged, pleomorphic, hyperchromatic nuclei with prominent nucleoli and moderate amounts of pale eosinophilic to amphophilic cytoplasm. Nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios are increased. Luminal necrosis (so-called "dirty necrosis") is focally identified within some glandular lumina. Nuclear contour irregularity and occasional mitotic figures (approximately 4 per 10 high-power fields) are present. The overall architecture is consistent with moderately differentiated (grade 2) carcinoma.

The surrounding stroma is markedly desmoplastic with dense collagenous fibrosis and embedded spindle-shaped fibroblasts and myofibroblasts, a hallmark of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. Scattered chronic inflammatory cells, predominantly lymphocytes, are present within the stroma. No granulomas are identified. No normal pancreatic acinar parenchyma is observed within the biopsy cores, suggesting complete replacement by tumor and reactive stroma at the sampled site.

No lymphovascular invasion is identified within the biopsy material (note: assessment limited by the nature of the specimen). No perineural invasion is identified in the sampled cores.

Cell block sections show similar atypical glandular clusters with the morphologic features described above. Background material includes blood and proteinaceous debris.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC):
Performed on formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded sections from the cell block and core biopsy material:

  CK7 (clone OV-TL 12/30):         POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells)
  CK19 (clone RCK108):              POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells)
  MUC1 (clone MA695):               POSITIVE (luminal and cytoplasmic, moderate to strong)
  CA 19-9 (clone 1116-NS-19-9):     POSITIVE (cytoplasmic, moderate)
  CK20 (clone Ks20.8):              NEGATIVE
  CDX2 (clone AMT28):               NEGATIVE
  TTF-1 (clone 8G7G3/1):            NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin (polyclonal):        NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A (polyclonal):       NEGATIVE
  Trypsin (polyclonal):              NEGATIVE
  Insulin (clone 2D11-H5):           NEGATIVE
  p53 (clone DO-7):                  ABERRANT OVEREXPRESSION (strong diffuse nuclear positivity in >80% of tumor nuclei; interpretation consistent with TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation)
  SMAD4/DPC4 (clone B-8):           LOSS OF EXPRESSION (absent nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells; internal control stromal cells positive)

  Ki-67 (clone MIB-1):              Approximately 35% nuclear labeling index in the neoplastic glandular component

INTERPRETATION OF IHC:
The immunoprofile (CK7+, CK19+, MUC1+, CA 19-9+, CK20-, CDX2-, TTF-1-, synaptophysin-, chromogranin-, trypsin-) is consistent with pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma of pancreatobiliary lineage. The neuroendocrine markers are negative, excluding a well-differentiated pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor. Trypsin negativity excludes acinar cell carcinoma as the primary diagnosis. The absence of SMAD4/DPC4 expression and the aberrant overexpression of p53 are common alterations in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma and are consistent with this diagnosis.

MOLECULAR / ANCILLARY STUDIES — STATUS:
  Microsatellite instability (MSI) by IHC (MLH1, PMS2, MSH2, MSH6):  PENDING — ordered; results to be issued as addendum upon completion
  KRAS mutational analysis (PCR/Sanger or NGS):                        PENDING — ordered; reflex to comprehensive NGS panel if adequate material
  Comprehensive somatic genomic profiling (tumor NGS, 648-gene panel):  PENDING — tissue forwarded to molecular pathology laboratory; anticipated turnaround 2-3 weeks; results to be issued as separate molecular pathology report

Note: If MSI IHC demonstrates loss of any mismatch repair protein, reflex PCR-based MSI testing will be performed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS:

  A.  EUS-GUIDED FINE NEEDLE BIOPSY, PANCREATIC HEAD MASS:

      MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA

      - Infiltrative moderately differentiated (Grade 2) ductal adenocarcinoma with desmoplastic stroma
      - Morphology consistent with conventional (tubular) pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
      - Acinar cell carcinoma excluded (trypsin negative)
      - Well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor excluded (synaptophysin and chromogranin negative)
      - SMAD4/DPC4: Loss of expression (see IHC above)
      - p53: Aberrant overexpression (consistent with TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation)
      - Ki-67 proliferation index: ~35%
      - Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified in submitted material
      - Perineural invasion: Not identified in submitted material
      - Mismatch repair IHC: PENDING (see ancillary studies)
      - Somatic NGS: PENDING (see ancillary studies)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATHOLOGIC STAGING NOTE:
This is a biopsy specimen; formal pathologic staging per AJCC is not applicable to this specimen. Staging will be determined following resection if performed.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The histologic and immunophenotypic features of this EUS-guided fine needle biopsy are diagnostic of moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma arising from the pancreatic head. The desmoplastic stromal reaction with infiltrative irregular glands and nuclear atypia, combined with the supporting IHC profile (CK7+, CK19+, MUC1+, CA 19-9+, CK20-, CDX2-, synaptophysin-, chromogranin-, trypsin-), is characteristic of conventional pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. Loss of SMAD4/DPC4 expression by IHC is identified in approximately 55% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas and is an adverse prognostic finding. Aberrant p53 overexpression is consistent with a TP53 missense gain-of-function mutation (most commonly R175H or R248W/Q), which is identified in approximately 70% of PDAC cases. Confirmatory molecular studies including MSI assessment and comprehensive somatic NGS are pending and will be reported as addenda.

The clinical and radiographic context (3.8 cm hypoenhancing pancreatic head mass with biliary and pancreatic ductal obstruction, markedly elevated CA 19-9, and imaging features of borderline resectable disease) is concordant with this histologic diagnosis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONIC SIGNATURE:
Attending Pathologist: [REDACTED], M.D., Ph.D.
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Report Status: FINAL (Ancillary studies pending; addendum to follow)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
